Gently nudges the prompt before reveal and Next after reveal if you pause.

Shows welcome-back tips. Time-since-last-conjugation messages still appear.

A A

A focused Italian verb trainer scaffold built for a serious v1 kickoff.

  • Italian sibling-repo scaffold on the Desktop
  • Starter verb seed with auxiliary and past-participle metadata
  • Early Italian tense focus: present, passato prossimo, imperfetto, future, conditional
  • Shared drill engine copied from the app family source
  • Room for usage nuggets, packaged audio, and larger datasets later

Progress and settings save automatically.

Italian Verb Notes & Tips

-ARE verbs

Pattern: -o, -i, -a, -iamo, -ate, -ano

Example: parlare → parlo, parli, parla, parliamo, parlate, parlano

-ERE verbs

Pattern: -o, -i, -e, -iamo, -ete, -ono

Example: prendere → prendo, prendi, prende, prendiamo, prendete, prendono

-IRE verbs

Pattern: -o, -i, -e, -iamo, -ite, -ono

Example: sentire → sento, senti, sente, sentiamo, sentite, sentono

High-value irregulars

Start with: essere, avere, fare, andare, venire, dire, dare, stare, potere, volere, dovere, sapere

Why: they dominate beginner usage and make `passato prossimo` and modal drills much more useful.

Ready?
Autosay is enabled.
Tap Continue to begin.

Quick Help

  • Tap almost anywhere to reveal the answer, then tap again to move on.
  • Use Settings to choose which tenses and which verb ranges are in play. If you are starting out, use Top 50 verbs with the most common tenses.
  • Hear plays the prompt before reveal and the answer after reveal. Say lets you answer aloud and get dictation feedback.
  • Usage shows or hides the example lines while you drill. Most Italian text is playable, including usages and verb details.
  • Search opens any verb and its full conjugation table.
